About: It is a contest for working tugboats.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with a dockside festival.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the category of contests.
ConsistsOf: It consists of nose-to-nose pushing competitions, a line toss competition, and best mascot/costume contests between the crews.
FoundedIn: It was founded in part by Captain Jerry Roberts.
HasA: It has a course of one nautical mile.
It has a line toss competition.
It has a knot tying competition for spectators.
It has an on land line toss competition for spectators.
It has a spinach eating competition for spectators.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by spinach eating competition in honor of Popeye the sailor cartoon character.
HeldIn: It was held at pier 63 Maritime.
HeldOn: It was held on Staten Island.
It was held on the Sunday before Labor Day.
KnownAs: It is known as the Great North River Tug Boat Race and Competition.
It is known as the New York Tug Boat Race.
It is known as the Hudson River Tugboat Race.
KnownFor: It is known for its spinach eating competition.
LocatedIn: It is located on the Hudson River.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the 79th Street Boat Basin.
PartOf: It is part of a dockside festival.
PerformedBy: It was performed by working tugboats.
UsedFor: It is used for award ceremonies.
It is used for spectator events.
It is used for musical entertainment.
UsedIn: It is used in the Fall.
